title: Bhature
description: Bhature is a soft, fluffy, deep-fried North Indian bread that is traditionally served with chole. Made from a lightly fermented dough, it is known for its airy texture, crisp exterior, and rich flavor.

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Plain yogurt (curd) – ¼ cup (helps with softness)
Oil or melted ghee (for dough) – 2 tbsp
Warm water – as needed (approx. ½ cup)

instruction.title: 1. Mix Dry Ingredients
steps: In a large bowl, sift together the all-purpose flour, semolina, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, and salt.

instruction.title: 2. Add Yogurt and Oil
steps: Add the yogurt and oil to the dry mixture. Mix well with your fingers or a spoon until crumbly.

instruction.title: 3. Knead the Dough
steps: Gradually add warm water little by little and knead to form a smooth, soft, and slightly sticky dough.
The dough should be softer than regular chapati dough.

instruction.title: 4. Rest the Dough
steps: Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for at least 2 hours at room temperature.
This fermentation gives bhature its characteristic fluffiness.

instruction.title: 5. Divide and Roll
steps: After resting, knead the dough briefly again.
Divide it into equal-sized balls (about lemon size).
Roll each ball into a smooth round disc about 5–6 inches in diameter.
Use minimal flour to dust while rolling.

instruction.title: 6. Heat Oil
steps: Heat oil in a deep frying pan or wok over medium-high heat.
The oil should be hot but not smoking (test by dropping a small piece of dough; it should rise immediately).

instruction.title: 7. Fry the Bhature
steps: Carefully slide the rolled dough into the hot oil.
Press gently with a slotted spoon to help it puff up.
Fry until golden brown on one side, then flip and fry the other side until golden and cooked through.

instruction.title: 8. Drain and Serve
steps: Remove with a slotted spoon and drain excess oil on paper towels.
Serve hot with chole or any curry.

chefTips: Resting the dough is essential for softness and puffiness.
Use fresh yogurt to aid fermentation.
Maintain consistent oil temperature — too hot burns bhature quickly, too cool makes it oily.
Press gently after dropping bhature into oil to encourage puffing.
